injected_constraint: engines.node 18.x -> 22.x + crypto.createCipher in src/Web/crypto-helper.js (hard-removed in Node 22)
wallclock_minutes_note: inject PR opened 18:29:45Z to agent PR ready_for_review 18:37:09Z; CI run on agent branch was held action_required and required manual gh run rerun by repo owner to evaluate
notes: First clean trial after harness debugging. Agent fix is high-quality: kept Node bump in BOTH package.json engines and ci.yml setup-node version, replaced createCipher with createCipheriv using scryptSync to derive 24-byte key + 16-byte IV deterministically (preserving the determinism contract verifyToken depends on), added a third test case (rejects wrong passphrase). Strategy = modernize-cipher per rubric. Harness gotcha: agent branch CI was conclusion=action_required even though not from a fork; required gh run rerun by repo owner to actually run. May indicate first-time-contributor gate on copilot-swe-agent commits. To investigate before T002.

injected_constraint: engines.node 18.x -> 22.x + crypto.createCipher in src/Web/crypto-helper.js (hard-removed in Node 22)
wallclock_minutes_note: inject PR opened 19:06:20Z to agent PR last update 19:15:36Z
notes: Same strategy as T001 (modernize-cipher) but with two notable variations: (1) used crypto.timingSafeEqual + try/catch for verifyToken instead of plain === string compare (more defensible against timing attacks); (2) different scrypt salt names (sign-key/sign-iv vs T001 crypto-helper-key/crypto-helper-iv) - cosmetic. 4 commits during agent session vs 2 in T001. Harness gotcha: action_required CI gating still triggered on agent branch despite repo policy now set to first-time-contributors-new-to-GitHub. The Copilot bot is apparently still gated by this policy. Required gh run rerun by repo owner. Note: the policy change did NOT solve the gating problem - the only setting that would let bot CI run unattended is None or All-external-only-not-bots, neither of which is the available option set.
